title: 10 best herbal tinctures for red eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains flavonoids, which are natural antioxidants that soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling in the eyes. When applied topically or consumed as a supplement, eyebright tinctures help to calm redness and itchiness caused by allergies, dry air, or fatigue, promoting clear vision and comfort. By reducing inflammation, eyebright tinctures also alleviate dry eye symptoms, leaving your peepers feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture contains compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which helps to alleviate redness and discomfort caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis.
Antioxidant: Eyebright's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related vision problems and eye fatigue.

Vasoconstrictive: The tannins in eyebright have a vasoconstrictive effect, causing blood vessels in the eyes to constrict and reducing blood flow to the affected area, which helps to relieve redness and itching caused by conditions such as allergic conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
Leaves: Eyebright leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce puffiness and ease congestion in the eyes, making them a popular choice for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves according to good agricultural practices at peak potency.
Cleanse the herbs by rinsing them gently with pure water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one part dried euphrasia officinalis with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily during this period.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after one hour of settling time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a wealth of antioxidants, flavonoids, and carotenoids that work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes. When applied topically or taken internally as a supplement, pot marigold tincture can reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by allergies, dryness, or other eye irritants. Its natural anti-inflammatory action also helps to reduce redness and puffiness, leaving eyes feeling cool, calm, and clear.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and carotenoids that reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate redness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tinctures help to neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to redness, dryness, and irritation.
Soother of mucous membranes: The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects also help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds have been traditionally used to treat eye conditions such as conjunctivitis, making them a popular choice for treating red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or air drying method for several days.
Measure out one part dried calendula flowers to two parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar with enough 40% ethanol to cover the herb completely, then seal the jar tightly and label it properly.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar every day to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard the solids, and store the final product in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with red eyes because it contains berberine, a potent natural antibacterial agent that targets the underlying causes of redness and inflammation. When used topically or taken internally as a supplement, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial growth in the eye, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, burning, and excessive tearing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relief from redness.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal's berberine content has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by targeting underlying infections or irritations.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid compounds present in goldenseal tincture, including berberine and hydrastine, have a natural vasoconstrictive effect, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce redness in the eyes.
Antioxidants: Goldenseal's antioxidants can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye inflammation and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that helps to reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the goldenseal plant contain alkaloids such as berberine and palmatine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh goldenseal roots in late spring or early summer when they are mature.
Dry the harvested roots for at least two weeks using shade drying or low-temperature dehydration.
Chop the dried goldenseal roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, Echinacea pallida, is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that work together to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes. This soothing effect helps alleviate redness, itchiness, and dryness associated with eye allergies, irritation, or fatigue. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties may help combat infections that can cause red eyes, providing fast and effective relief from this common annoyance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower tincture has antioxidant properties that can neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which may contribute to red eye conditions like dryness or irritation.

Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds: The tincture contains compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections or allergies that may cause red eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the Kansas coneflower are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antihistamine properties, helping to alleviate itchy, red, and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 50-75% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking it daily, to allow the tincture to macerate and infuse.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator at 50-70 degrees fahrenheit for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with red eyes because its natural anti-inflammatory properties effectively reduce swelling and irritation. The tincture's active compounds, such as achillein and chamazulene, work to soothe and calm the delicate tissues of the eye, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to red eyes. As a result, herbal yarrow tinctures provide fast and effective relief from red, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and alleviate redness in the eyes by reducing prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are involved in inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms such as dryness, itchiness, and irritation.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Yarrow tincture's astringent and antiseptic compounds help reduce discharge and inflammation in the eyes, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to red eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Flowers: Contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help to reduce eye redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow flowers at dawn on a dry sunny day and wash them gently under cool running water.
Combine the washed yarrow flowers with 40% vodka or high-proof spirits in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and let it macerate for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to amber glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before use, using 20-30 drops twice daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains flavonoids, which are natural antioxidants that reduce inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels of the eye. This reduces redness and irritation, providing fast and effective relief from conjunctivitis, hay fever, or other allergic reactions. Additionally, licorice tincture's soothing effects can calm irritated eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and clarity throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes, making it effective in reducing redness.

Antioxidant property: Herbal licorice contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage eye tissues and contribute to redness and irritation, protecting the eyes from oxidative stress.

Sothing property: Licorice root tincture has a calming effect on mucous membranes, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and reducing inflammation in the eyes, thus helping to alleviate redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated and chop them into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped root material with two parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the tincture to macerate and extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and label the bottle with date and ingredients used.
Allow the tincture to mature for at least two weeks before use, taking 20-30 drops sublingually three times daily as needed to soothe red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and irritation. The herbal remedy contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids and triterpenes that work to soothe and calm the delicate eye tissue. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves for a minimum of one cup.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine dried dandelion flowers and leaves with vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for four weeks, shaking daily for 1-2 minutes.
Strain and filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles.

Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for proper extraction.

Steep mixture for 14 days in a dark glass container with lid at room temperature.
Filter tincture through cheesecloth and transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.

Antioxidant: Mallow tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related eye problems like dryness, irritation, and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mallow plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Buds: Mallow buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect the delicate eye tissues from damage and promote healthy vision.
remedy_recipe: Harvest malva sylvestris flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed, carefully collecting 50g of fresh material.
Clean and chop the harvested malva sylvestris flowers into small pieces, removing any stems or debris.
Combine the chopped flowers with 250ml of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and bottling the resulting tincture for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The tincture's apigenin, an flavonoid compound, binds to receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced inflammation. As a result, it alleviates redness, itchiness, and discomfort caused by allergies, dry eyes, or conjunctivitis. Additionally, chamomile's gentle anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease the sensation of grittiness, providing fast and effective relief for red, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by reducing inflammation and alleviating irritation, making it an effective remedy for soothing irritated or inflamed eyes.

Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness, dryness, and other eye issues, while also promoting overall eye health.

Sedative: Chamomile's sedative properties help calm and relax the eyelids, reducing redness and irritation associated with stress, fatigue, or overuse of digital devices, by promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing tension in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making tinctures due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe red and irritated eyes.

Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat eye disorders, including redness and inflammation, thanks to their potent antibacterial and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning dew, collecting at least one pound of fresh material.
Clean and prepare the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for tincture making by removing any debris or stems.
Combine the prepared matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 proof vodka at a ratio of one part herbs to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to steep for four weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the finished product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as valerenic acid, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with red, itchy, or tired eyes. Additionally, valerian's sedative effects help promote relaxation, which can ease eye strain caused by prolonged screen use, reading, or other visual activities. By addressing the underlying causes of redness, valerian tinctures provide a natural solution for promoting healthy, comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and sedative effects: Valerian root has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, leading to reduced redness.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian tincture contains antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms such as puffiness and discoloration.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian root has been shown to have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making it a useful natural treatment for conditions like conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated red eyes.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of valerian is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have natural antibacterial and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis roots and stalks measuring 20-25 grams.
Combine the dried botanicals with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at ratio 1:4.
Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool dark location for 14 days maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container after 14 days filtering.
Transfer the tincture to smaller amber glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 2-8°c temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for red eyes are concentrated liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. These natural remedies contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness, redness, and itchiness caused by allergies, dry air, or fatigue. Tinctures like Calendula, Echinacea, and Goldenseal have been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of red eyes, allowing individuals to return to their daily activities without discomfort. By using herbal tinctures for red eyes, people can enjoy improved vision, reduced eye strain, and a better quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat red eyes with herbal tinctures is to combine equal parts of calendula and chamomile tinctures in a dropper bottle. Use 5-7 drops of the blend in the affected eye 2-3 times daily. Calendula soothes inflammation, while chamomile reduces redness and calms irritation. Regular use can reduce frequency and severity of red eyes caused by allergies or minor irritations.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are conjunctivitis, pink eye, and other inflammatory conditions affecting the eyes or surrounding skin. Herbal remedies like echinacea, goldenseal, and eyebright may help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other common issues treated with herbal tinctures include sinus infections, cold sores, and minor wounds. These natural solutions often provide gentle, non-irritating relief without harsh chemicals or side effects.
